codeReview.summary: The migration from JWT to session-cookie auth is broadly implemented across app/api middleware, auth endpoints, SSE auth, and server tests. However, one security-critical issue remains in session secret handling.

issues.description: Session signing secret falls back to a hardcoded constant ("change-me") when SESSION_SECRET and JWT_SECRET are unset. This can allow predictable cookie signing in misconfigured environments. The feature spec requires env-based secrets (SESSION_SECRET with JWT_SECRET migration fallback), so this should fail fast instead of using a known default.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS auth bootstrap guidance is internally inconsistent after the session-auth migration.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/andy/.factory/missions/80c5e196-cca5-4acd-9581-c44e4271690a/AGENTS.md:72 still says login returns a JWT token, while the same file also documents session-cookie auth in the Session Cookie Auth Migration section.

sharedStateObservations.observation: implementation-worker skill contains outdated validator guidance about known failing scheduledChanges tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/org-planner/.factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md:57 says there are 4 pre-existing scheduledChanges failures, but the worker verification reports those tests passing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture library doc still describes backend auth as JWT/jsonwebtoken, which no longer matches the current session-cookie server architecture.

summary: Review result: fail. Session-cookie migration coverage is strong, but a blocking security issue exists due to hardcoded session-secret fallback. Also noted shared-state documentation/skill drift around JWT-vs-session guidance.
